name: pyhelm3

metadata.name: pyhelm3
metadata.summary: Python library for managing Helm releases using Helm 3.

documentation:
# pyhelm3

Python library for managing Helm releases using Helm 3 (i.e. Tiller-less Helm).

## Installation

`pyhelm3` can be installed from PyPI:

```sh
pip install pyhelm3
```

## Usage

```python
from pyhelm3 import Client


# This will use the Kubernetes configuration from the environment
client = Client()
# Specify the kubeconfig file to use
client = Client(kubeconfig = "/path/to/kubeconfig")
# Specify the kubecontext to use
client = Client(kubecontext = "kubecontext")
# Specify a custom Helm executable (by default, we expect 'helm' to be on the PATH)
client = Client(executable = "/path/to/helm")


# List the deployed releases
releases = await client.list_releases(all = True, all_namespaces = True)
for release in releases:
    revision = await release.current_revision()
    print(release.name, release.namespace, revision.revision, str(revision.status))


# Get the current revision for an existing release
revision = await client.get_current_revision("cert-manager", namespace = "cert-manager")
chart_metadata = await revision.chart_metadata()
print(
    revision.release.name,
    revision.release.namespace,
    revision.revision,
    str(revision.status),
    chart_metadata.name,
    chart_metadata.version
)


# Fetch a chart
chart = await client.get_chart(
    "cert-manager",
    repo = "https://charts.jetstack.io",
    version = "v1.8.x"
)
print(chart.metadata.name, chart.metadata.version)
print(await chart.readme())


# Install or upgrade a release
revision = await client.install_or_upgrade_release(
    "cert-manager",
    chart,
    { "installCRDs": True },
    atomic = True,
    wait = True
)
print(
    revision.release.name,
    revision.release.namespace,
    revision.revision,
    str(revision.status)
)


# Uninstall a release
#   Via the revision
revision = await client.get_current_revision("cert-manager", namespace = "cert-manager")
await revision.release.uninstall(wait = True)
#   Or directly by name
await client.uninstall_release("cert-manager", namespace = "cert-manager", wait = True)
```

classes.name: pyhelm3.errors.Error
documentation: Raised when an error occurs with a Helm command.

classes.name: pyhelm3.errors.ConnectionError
documentation: Raised when there is a problem connecting to the Kubernetes API.

classes.name: pyhelm3.errors.ChartNotFoundError
documentation: Raised when a chart is not found.

classes.name: pyhelm3.errors.FailedToRenderChartError
documentation: Raised when a chart fails to render.

classes.name: pyhelm3.errors.ReleaseNotFoundError
documentation: Raised when a release is not found.

classes.name: pyhelm3.errors.ResourceAlreadyExistsError
documentation: Raised when Helm attempts to create a resource that already exists.

classes.name: pyhelm3.errors.InvalidResourceError
documentation: Raised when Helm attempts to create or update a resource in a way that is not valid.

classes.name: pyhelm3.errors.CommandCancelledError
documentation: Raised when a Helm command is cancelled.

functions.name: pyhelm3.client.mergeconcat
documentation: Deep-merge two or more dictionaries together. Lists are concatenated.

classes.name: pyhelm3.client.Client

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.get_chart
documentation: Returns the resolved chart for the given ref, repo and version.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.pull_chart
documentation:
Context manager that pulls the specified chart and yields a chart object
whose ref is the unpacked chart directory.

Ensures that the directory is cleaned up when the context manager exits.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.template_resources
documentation:
Renders the templates from the given chart with the given values and returns
the resources that would be produced.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.list_releases
documentation: Returns an iterable of the deployed releases.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.get_current_revision
documentation: Returns the current revision of the named release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.install_or_upgrade_release
documentation:
Install or upgrade the named release using the given chart and values and return
the new revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.get_proceedable_revision
documentation:
Returns a proceedable revision for the named release by rolling back or deleting
as appropriate where the release has been left in a pending state.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.should_install_or_upgrade_release
documentation:
Returns True if an install or upgrade is required based on the given revision,
chart and values, False otherwise.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.ensure_release
documentation:
Ensures the named release matches the given chart and values and return the current
revision.

It the release must be rolled back or deleted in order to be proceedable, this method
will ensure that happens. It will also only make a new release if the chart and/or
values have changed.

methods.name: pyhelm3.client.Client.uninstall_release
documentation: Uninstall the named release.
Entrypoint for interactions with Helm.

classes.name: pyhelm3.models.Chart

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Chart.ref_is_abspath
documentation: If the ref is a path on the filesystem, make sure it is absolute.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Chart._run_command

documentation: Returns the README for the chart.

classes.name: pyhelm3.models.Release

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Release.current_revision
documentation: Returns the current revision for the release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Release.revision
documentation: Returns the specified revision for the release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Release.history
documentation: Returns all the revisions for the release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Release.rollback
documentation:
Rollback this release to the specified version and return the resulting revision.

If no revision is specified, it will rollback to the previous release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Release.simulate_rollback
documentation: Simulate a rollback to the specified revision and return the diff.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Release.simulate_upgrade
documentation: Simulate a rollback to the specified revision and return the diff.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Release.upgrade
documentation: Upgrade this release using the given chart and values and return the new revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.Release.uninstall
documentation: Uninstalls this release.
Model for a Helm release.

classes.name: pyhelm3.models.HookDeletePolicy
documentation: Enumeration of possible delete policies for a hook.

classes.name: pyhelm3.models.HookPhase
documentation: Enumeration of possible phases for a hook.

classes.name: pyhelm3.models.Hook

classes.name: pyhelm3.models.ReleaseRevision

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.ReleaseRevision.chart_metadata
documentation: Returns the metadata for the chart that was used for this revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.ReleaseRevision.hooks
documentation: Returns the hooks that were executed as part of this revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.ReleaseRevision.resources
documentation: Returns the resources that were created as part of this revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.ReleaseRevision.values
documentation: Returns the values that were used for this revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.ReleaseRevision.refresh
documentation: Returns a new revision representing the most recent state of this revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.ReleaseRevision.diff
documentation: Returns the diff between this revision and the specified revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.models.ReleaseRevision._from_status
documentation: Internal constructor to create a release revision from a status result.
Model for a revision of a release.

classes.name: pyhelm3.command.SafeLoader
documentation:
We use a custom YAML loader that doesn't bork on plain equals '=' signs.

It was originally designated with a special meaning, but noone uses it:

    https://github.com/yaml/pyyaml/issues/89
    https://yaml.org/type/value.html

classes.name: pyhelm3.command.Command

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.run
documentation: Run the given Helm command with the given input as stdin and

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.diff_release
documentation: Returns the diff between two releases created from the same chart.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.diff_revision
documentation:
Returns the diff between two revisions of the specified release.

If the second revision is not specified, the latest revision is used.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.diff_rollback
documentation:
Returns the diff that would result from rolling back the given release
to the specified revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.diff_upgrade
documentation:
Returns the diff that would result from rolling back the given release
to the specified revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.diff_version
documentation: Returns the version of the Helm diff plugin (https://github.com/databus23/helm-diff).

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.get_chart_metadata
documentation: Returns metadata for the chart that was used to deploy the release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.get_hooks
documentation: Returns the hooks for the specified release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.get_resources
documentation: Returns the resources for the specified release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.get_values
documentation:
Returns the values for the specified release.

Optionally, the full computed values can be requested.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.history
documentation:
Returns the historical revisions for the specified release.

The maximum number of revisions to return can be specified (defaults to 256).

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.install_or_upgrade
documentation: Installs or upgrades the specified release using the given chart and values.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.list
documentation: Returns the list of releases that match the given options.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.pull
documentation:
Fetch a chart from a remote location and unpack it locally.

Returns the path of the directory into which the chart was downloaded and unpacked.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.repo_list
documentation: Lists the available Helm repositories.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.repo_add
documentation:
Adds a repository to the available Helm repositories.

Returns the new repo list on success.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.repo_update
documentation:
Updates the chart indexes for the specified repositories.

If no repositories are given, all repositories are updated.

Returns the repo list on success.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.repo_remove
documentation:
Removes the specified chart.

Returns the new repo list on success.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.rollback
documentation: Rollback the specified release to the specified revision.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.search
documentation: Search the available Helm repositories for charts matching the specified constraints.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.show_chart
documentation: Returns the contents of Chart.yaml for the specified chart.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.show_crds
documentation: Returns the CRDs for the specified chart.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.show_readme
documentation: Returns the README for the specified chart.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.show_values
documentation: Returns the default values for the specified chart.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.status
documentation: Get the status of the specified release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.template
documentation: Renders the chart templates and returns the resources.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.uninstall
documentation: Uninstall the specified release.

methods.name: pyhelm3.command.Command.version
documentation: Returns the Helm version.
Class presenting an async interface around the Helm CLI.
